title: ` __pypy__.intop.int_mulmod(1,0,0)` will abort with a Fatal RPython error
author: devdanzin
body:
It's possible to abort PyPy by calling `__pypy__.intop.int_mulmod` with invalid values:

```python
Python 3.10.14 (5cccf8d934d7, Nov 17 2024, 04:47:11)
[PyPy 7.3.18-alpha0 with GCC 10.2.1 20210130 (Red Hat 10.2.1-11)] on linux
>>>> import __pypy__.intop
>>>> __pypy__.intop.int_mulmod(1,0,0)
RPython traceback:
  File "pypy_interpreter.c", line 49327, in BuiltinCode3_fastcall_3
  File "implement_6.c", line 58858, in fastfunc_int_mulmod_3
Fatal RPython error: AssertionError
Aborted
```
Found using fusil by @vstinner.
